name: The White House
subject: Statement from President Donald J. Trump
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2ad8fcb1e1c15caeaf0ad48e10fd4ce447351660c87802072e5f0884fdd0d01314387a60edfef766e5e49dc3148bf5879a7a71ac31ce1b33

STATEMENT FROM PRESIDENT DONALD J. TRUMP

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2ad8fcb1e1c15cae4c8cf25c8549910c33abdb2a7aba3dbb7b51394a08884928162454f13d261050911c04912569bfec2c5d5af96a62df43

My fellow Americans, today I speak to a nation in grief. Yesterday, a school filled with innocent children and caring teachers became the scene of terrible violence, hatred, and evil.

Around 2:30 yesterday afternoon, police responded to reports of gunfire at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida -- a great and safe community. There, a shooter, who is now in custody, opened fire on defenseless students and teachers. He murdered 17 people and badly wounded at least 14 others.

Our entire nation, with one heavy heart, is praying for the victims and their families. To every parent, teacher, and child who is hurting so badly, we are here for you -- whatever you need, whatever we can do, to ease your pain. We are all joined together as one American family, and your suffering is our burden also.

No child, no teacher, should ever be in danger in an American school. No parent should ever have to fear for their sons and daughters when they kiss them goodbye in the morning.

Each person who was stolen from us yesterday had a full life ahead of them -- a life filled with wondrous beauty and unlimited potential and promise. Each one had dreams to pursue, love to give, and talents to share with the world. And each one had a family to whom they meant everything in the world.

Today, we mourn for all of those who lost their lives. We comfort the grieving and the wounded. And we hurt for the entire community of Parkland, Florida that is now in shock, in pain, and searching for answers.

To law enforcement, first responders, and teachers who responded so bravely in the face of danger: We thank you for your courage. Soon after the shooting, I spoke with Governor Scott to convey our deepest sympathies to the people of Florida and our determination to assist in any way that we can. I also spoke with Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi and Broward County Sheriff Scott Israel.

I'm making plans to visit Parkland to meet with families and local officials, and to continue coordinating the federal response.

In these moments of heartache and darkness, we hold on to God’s word in scripture: “I have heard your prayer and seen your tears. I will heal you.”

We trust in that promise, and we hold fast to our fellow Americans in their time of sorrow.

I want to speak now directly to America’s children, especially those who feel lost, alone, confused or even scared: I want you to know that you are never alone and you never will be. You have people who care about you, who love you, and who will do anything at all to protect you. If you need help, turn to a teacher, a family member, a local police officer, or a faith leader. Answer hate with love; answer cruelty with kindness.

We must also work together to create a culture in our country that embraces the dignity of life, that creates deep and meaningful human connections, and that turns classmates and colleagues into friends and neighbors.

Our administration is working closely with local authorities to investigate the shooting and learn everything we can. We are committed to working with state and local leaders to help secure our schools, and tackle the difficult issue of mental health.

Later this month, I will be meeting with the nation’s governors and attorney generals, where making our schools and our children safer will be our top priority. It is not enough to simply take actions that make us feel like we are making a difference. We must actually make that difference.

In times of tragedy, the bonds that sustain us are those of family, faith, community, and country. These bonds are stronger than the forces of hatred and evil, and these bonds grow even stronger in the hours of our greatest need.

And so always, but especially today, let us hold our loved ones close, let us pray for healing and for peace, and let us come together as one nation to wipe away the tears and strive for a much better tomorrow.

Thank you. And God Bless you all. Thank you very much.

Yesterday, a school filled with innocent children and caring teachers became the scene of terrible violence, hatred, and evil. Around 2:30 yesterday afternoon, police responded to reports of gunfire at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida -- a great and safe community. There, a shooter, who is now in custody, opened fire on defenseless students and teachers. He murdered 17 people and badly wounded at least 14 others. Our entire nation, with one heavy heart, is praying for the victims and their families. To every parent, teacher, and child who is hurting so badly, we are here for you -- whatever you need, whatever we can do, to ease your pain. We are all joined together as one American family, and your suffering is our burden also. No child, no teacher, should ever be in danger in an American school. No parent should ever have to fear for their sons and daughters when they kiss them goodbye in the morning. Each person who was stolen from us yesterday had a full life ahead of them -- a life filled with wondrous beauty and unlimited potential and promise. Each one had dreams to pursue, love to give, and talents to share with the world. And each one had a family to whom they meant everything in the world. Today, we mourn for all of those who lost their lives. We comfort the grieving and the wounded. And we hurt for the entire community of Parkland, Florida that is now in shock, in pain, and searching for answers. To law enforcement, first responders, and teachers who responded so bravely in the face of danger: We thank you for your courage. Soon after the shooting, I spoke with Governor Scott to convey our deepest sympathies to the people of Florida and our determination to assist in any way that we can. I also spoke with Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi and Broward County Sheriff Scott Israel. I'm making plans to visit Parkland to meet with families and local officials, and to continue coordinating the federal response. In these moments of heartache and darkness, we hold on to God’s word in scripture: “I have heard your prayer and seen your tears. I will heal you.” We trust in that promise, and we hold fast to our fellow Americans in their time of sorrow. I want to speak now directly to America’s children, especially those who feel lost, alone, confused or even scared: I want you to know that you are never alone and you never will be. You have people who care about you, who love you, and who will do anything at all to protect you. If you need help, turn to a teacher, a family member, a local police officer, or a faith leader. Answer hate with love; answer cruelty with kindness. We must also work together to create a culture in our country that embraces the dignity of life, that creates deep and meaningful human connections, and that turns classmates and colleagues into friends and neighbors. Our administration is working closely with local authorities to investigate the shooting and learn everything we can. We are committed to working with state and local leaders to help secure our schools, and tackle the difficult issue of mental health. Later this month, I will be meeting with the nation’s governors and attorney generals, where making our schools and our children safer will be our top priority. It is not enough to simply take actions that make us feel like we are making a difference. We must actually make that difference. In times of tragedy, the bonds that sustain us are those of family, faith, community, and country. These bonds are stronger than the forces of hatred and evil, and these bonds grow even stronger in the hours of our greatest need. And so always, but especially today, let us hold our loved ones close, let us pray for healing and for peace, and let us come together as one nation to wipe away the tears and strive for a much better tomorrow. Thank you. And God Bless you all. Thank you very much.

subject: A message from Gabby Giffords about gun violence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
|
---

| Activist - I know politicians fear the gun lobby.

But I also know that fear cannot compare to what those innocent children in Florida felt as the gunshots rang out, bullets flew through the halls of their school, and their teachers and classmates were gunned down.

That fear is nothing compared to what parents must have felt waiting in the parking lot of the high school waiting for news about their children.

If I could offer protection to those families and the 315 Americans who are shot every day, I would. But we can't wall ourselves from the threat of gun violence, nor will we find safety in the deadly cycle of arming ourselves against each other.

The defenders of the status quo - advocates of the firearms industry and the politicians paid to defend it - will tell you that events like these are virtual acts of nature, products of mental illness or bad parenting, beyond our ability to control.

This could not be further from the truth.

Every day we fail to take action, we choose this fate. We tolerate politicians who fail to acknowledge this crisis and vote against our safety. We let our gun violence epidemic continue day after deadly day.

That cycle has to end, but it will only happen when politicians realize they have more to fear from you than they do the gun lobby. And that starts with making your voice heard.

Sign my pledge:

[**Add Your Name: "I promise you that if we cannot make our communities safer from gun violence with the Congress we have now, I will use every means available to make sure we have a different Congress, one that puts communities' interests ahead of the gun lobby's. I will Vote Courage in November and support candidates who will stand up to the gun lobby and take action to make our communities safer."**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/WhDpF/6/dD/r5cs/6RgJjr/GNT7PQPn3/P/P)

My heart is with the victims and survivors, and my gratitude is with Broward County's courageous first responders. The question now is if we will find the courage to pass the laws we need to protect our children, to stop dangerous people from accessing guns.

If Congress won't act, American voters must. All my best, Gabby Giffords [SIGN MY PLEDGE](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/WhDpF/s/dD/r5cs/6RgJjr/v4S79m52GT/P/P "http://action.americansforresponsiblesolutions.org/page/s/ars-enough?source=em171002-c")

name: NHGOP Press & Communications
subject: Forrester Statement on Governor Sununu's State of the State Address
body:
**Forrester Statement on Governor Sununu's State of the State Address**

**For Immediate Release:** 2/15/2018
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement following Governor Chris Sununu's State of the State Address:
“Gov. Chris Sununu’s policies have resulted in lower taxes and fewer regulations on businesses large and small. The result? New Hampshire has the fastest growing economy in New England. The Sununu administration has put more money into schools and provided more education options for families. Gov. Sununu has directed $38 million to local cities and towns for infrastructure improvements. And he has signed legislation to strengthen our Second Amendment rights and protect our ballot integrity. The state of the state of New Hampshire? Stronger than ever!”

###

name: Tom Perez
subject: I want to talk about gun violence, Derek
body:
It's long past time for our leaders to stop pretending we are helpless in the face of such tragedy.
---
Derek --

Yesterday afternoon, a 19-year-old with a weapon of war walked into Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida and opened fire, killing 17 people and injuring many others.

We have seen this happen too many times. This is not normal. This is not acceptable. This is not inevitable. It's long past time for our leaders to stop pretending we are helpless in the face of such tragedy.

Let's be very clear about something. The vast majority of Americans support common-sense solutions to prevent this type of gun violence. But the special interests that stand against measures like universal background checks are ruthless -- and they're also better organized.

Each election cycle, the NRA spends tens of millions of dollars to defeat candidates who will stand against their extreme agenda -- and as a result, many Republican elected officials owe their election in part to support from the gun lobby. So it's no surprise why gun violence prevention legislation never makes any progress in Congress.

Enough is enough, Derek. What we're letting happen right now in America is madness. Our children and our country deserve better.

We deserve a Congress that is willing to take up this debate. We deserve more than thoughts and prayers when these tragedies happen. We deserve leaders who understand the urgent need to take action on this issue.

Changing our gun laws won't stop every mass shooting -- but it will stop some of them. And we _have_ to try.

There are solutions that can address our nation's epidemic of gun violence -- such as expanding background checks and closing the gun-show loophole. Now we just need to elect a Congress with the courage to stand up to the gun lobby.

We must not become numb to these horrendous mass shootings. We must keep fighting.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

P.S. It is up to all of us to demand action on this issue. **[Add your name to tell Congress it's past time to take action to address our country's gun violence epidemic.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405221a4/4067b0a/55ca99afa/7e552440/2434447234/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/gun-violence?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+tell+Congress+its+past+&utm_campaign=em_20180215_tp_s2&source=em_20180215_tp_s2")**

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405221a4/4067b0a/55ca99afa/7e552441/2434447234/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180215_tp_s2&source=em_20180215_tp_s2"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405221a4/4067b0a/55ca99afa/7e55244e/2434447234/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180215_tp_s2&source=em_20180215_tp_s2"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405221a4/4067b0a/55ca99afa/7e55244f/2434447234/VEsC/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180215_tp_s2&source=em_20180215_tp_s2") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405221a4/4067b0a/55ca99afa/7e55244c/2434447234/VEsD/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180215_tp_s2&source=em_20180215_tp_s2"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

body:

One of my favorite parts of being Pennsylvania's governor is meeting people from all walks of life and listening to what's on their minds.

That's why I've set aside time to get dinner with the people who make this campaign strong -- people like you -- so we can keep fighting for the issues that you care about.

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: Do you know what happened to Coach Kennedy?
body:
| | The stand for religious liberty is now
---
[ ](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/e180215apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180215apl01nd&n=1)
| Dear Peter, After every game, Coach Kennedy would wait until his high school football players cleared the field before taking a knee to silently thank God in a prayer. It was this 15-second silent prayer that had him suspended by the school district and later fired from his job. **It’s hard to believe this happened in the United States. And if we don’t mobilize millions of Christians to stand in defense of religious liberty at the polls this year – then we can expect to see more legal cases like Coach Kennedy’s.** You and I must do something to protect our religious freedoms, and engage Christians to stand boldly like never before – and it starts at the voting booth. If the 90-million Christians in America put their faith to work at the ballot box we can ensure religious liberty is protected through our school boards, our appellate courts, supreme courts, state legislators, governors and leaders in Washington, DC! Together we can do this! We are mobilizing millions of Christians, and we need your help. [Please prayerfully consider making your best donation to My Faith Votes today to help us engage more Christians before the elections.](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/e180215apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180215apl01nd&n=2) I’m asking you to stand with me for the future of religious liberty in America for our children and our grandchildren. I hope I can count on you to help us reach more Christians before the upcoming elections. God Bless You,
Mike Huckabee [](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/e180215apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180215apl01nd&n=3)

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Commonsense gun reform
body:
Yesterday, another U.S. city, Parkland, Florida, was faced with the unimaginable tragedy
#
Friends,
Yesterday, another U.S. city, Parkland, Florida, was faced with the unimaginable tragedy of a mass shooting. There have been 18 school shootings and 30 mass shootings in the United States in 2018.
Everyone with a conscience needs to speak up right now to stop the bloodshed.
[**Add your name to demand immediate action from Congress on commonsense gun reform.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad38/bed43eb/492a8ea1/75fee0b8/3836338176/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/commonsense-gun-reform?source=em180215")
Children, churches, schools, workplaces, concerts, movie theaters, and nightclubs have become targets of violence. What were once sanctuaries are no longer safe.
We have been shaken to our very cores again, but we cannot let grief turn into disbelief or apathy. **We. Must. Act.**
We must protect the precious lives of our neighbors and loved ones with a movement of conscience and compassion.
[**Demand Congress take immediate action on commonsense gun reform.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad38/bed43eb/492a8ea1/75fee0b8/3836338176/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/commonsense-gun-reform?source=em180215")
Action is our only acceptable path forward to honor the sacred bond we share as a people, to protect and care for one another in times of distress and danger. This is such a time.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution
**[ADD YOUR NAME»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad38/bed43eb/492a8ea1/75fee0b8/3836338176/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/commonsense-gun-reform?source=em180215")**
[**P.S. If you live in the South Florida area please consider participating in the blood drive to help trauma patients.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad38/bed43eb/492a8ea1/75fee0bb/3836338176/VEsC/ "https://www.oneblood.org/")

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Never "too soon"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31a5/9e00690/421d9323/2403b24c/4280583262/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/")
Hello,
Yesterday, families sent their children and loved ones to a place of learning with every intention of them returning home -- but now they are left with a loss so cruel it defies description.

Seventeen lives full of promise and potential have ended far too soon, and we deeply mourn with their families, friends, and community. And, while I am praying for them, I am not satisfied with that singular act.

It is never "too soon" to talk about gun violence, only "too late" \-- but once again, we were too late for a community. Chillingly so, I had this exact conversation at the debate with Speaker Corcoran the night before this horrendous massacre. If Republicans in our state are as concerned as they say they are with keeping our citizens safe, then now is the time to take action.
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31a5/9e00690/421d9323/2403b24f/4280583262/VEsE/ "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xR6NL93_QyQ")
[SHARE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31a5/9e00690/421d9323/2403b24f/4280583262/VEsF/ "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xR6NL93_QyQ")
I am not satisfied with the status quo of politicians sending thoughts and prayers to victims of gun violence, while they cash campaign checks from the gun lobby. Which is why when I'm Governor, I will introduce bills that:
* Ban assault weapons and large capacity magazines
* Ban bump stocks
* Strengthen and require universal background checks for all gun sales
* Restrict gun access for the mentally ill
* Close private-sale loopholes
* Restrict gun access for known foreign and domestic terrorists
* Close the "Boyfriend Loophole" and prohibit gun possession for those with felony or misdemeanor domestic violence and stalking convictions, and protection from abuse orders
* Ban the purchase and possession of armor-piercing bullets

These are steps our elected officials should take to ensure that more lives are not senselessly lost.

I am not satisfied with politicians who decry mental health's role in these mass shootings and do nothing to address the fact that they would happen less frequently with fewer weapons on our streets. I am not satisfied with those who lack the courage to follow their condemnation of these acts with equally strong actions to stop their occurrence.

I am heartbroken over this needless bloodshed in our state and our country and as Governor, I will work with anyone, and do everything possible, to stop them. This does not have to continue to be the norm.

I want better for my children, and for yours.

Andrew

Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

Tallahassee, FL 32303
